input:
Argument:
Cities should make buses and trains free because paying for things is annoying. Nobody likes swiping a card or digging for change, and it slows everyone down. Free transit would make people happy, and happy people are good for a city. Also, lots of other things are free, like parks and libraries, so transit should be free too. Honestly, if the city has enough money for stadiums it can afford this. It just makes sense and would obviously be better for everyone.

Please give me your honest comments and feedback on this argument.

Honestly, if the city has enough money for stadiums it can afford this. It just makes sense and would obviously be better for everyone.

input:
Argument:
We should fix climate change because the weather has been really weird lately and that's scary. Everyone keeps talking about it, so it must be a huge deal. If we just used less plastic and recycled more, the planet would basically be fine. Big companies cause most of the problem anyway, so regular people shouldn't have to worry too much. Besides, nature always finds a way to balance itself out, so things will probably work out as long as we care a little. It just feels wrong to pollute, and doing the right thing is always worth it.

Please give me your honest comments and feedback on this argument.

input:
Argument:
You should always trust your gut when it comes to relationships, because feelings don't lie. If someone is right for you, you'll just know it deep down, and overthinking only ruins things. My friend ignored her doubts and it worked out great, so clearly listening to your heart is the way to go. Relationships are like plants -- if it's meant to grow, it will, and if it isn't, no amount of effort matters. At the end of the day, love is a feeling, and you can't argue with a feeling.

Please give me your honest comments and feedback on this argument.
